title: Work Clothing - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Basic Needs > Work Clothing 11 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Women DRESS FOR SUCCESS OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides work clothing and employment support services. Work-Ready Clothing: Interview and work-ready clothing to help individuals transition into the workforce. Clients receive five pieces of work-ready clothing plus shoes, a coat, and accessories when they have a job interview, and five additional pieces of clothing when they get a job. Job Preparation: Provides job preparation classes for job seekers called Fast Track. Classes are currently online only. The regular cycle of four classes repeats monthly January-November. Additional services are available once Fast Track is completed. Employment Retention: The Professional Women's Group (PWG) is an ongoing group providing the opportunity for individuals to virtually meet with working women. Includes presentations and workshops to support life and career. The PWG meets regularly for programming with speakers from the community. Examples of program themes include financial education and planning, career development, the written and unwritten rules of the workplace, health and wellness, communication, and leadership skills. 1532 NE 37th Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Women and gender expansive individuals referred by a member agency Hours: Services: By appointment Job preparation: Thursday 10am-11:30am Email (503) 249-7300 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ELEVATE HIM PROFESSIONAL CLOTHING AND HAIRCUTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides interview and work-ready clothing to help with the transition into the workforce and for appointments that require a professional appearance. Also provides professional haircuts. Clothing donations accepted. 1513 SW Marlow Avenue Suite A Portland, OR 97225 Eligibility: Males age 12 and older Hours: Monday-Friday 10am-5pm by appointment Email (503) 810-1607 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GOODWILL INDUSTRIES OF LANE AND SOUTH COAST COUNTIES GOODWILL WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT VETERAN CONNECTIONS HOMELESS VETERAN REINTEGRATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides employment-related services to reintegrate homeless veterans into the labor force. Services include job search training and placement, employment readiness training, career exploration, and vocational/technical training. Also provides information about community resources, including housing, transportation, identification, clothing, computer access, and more. 855 Seneca Road Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: -Must be homeless or at risk of becoming homeless, or have been homeless within 60 days prior to enrollment -Must have served at least one day of active military service and have been discharged under any status other than dishonorable Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 431-3339 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS RESOURCES CLOTHES THAT WORK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance with acquiring appropriate clothing for individuals seeking employment, attending a job interview, or just starting new employment. 1755 13th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday/Saturday 9am-noon, closed holidays Email (503) 364-9936 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HELPING HANDS RESOURCES WORK WEAR Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides employer-required clothing and footwear suitable for positions in agriculture, construction, restaurant, landscaping, or nursing environments. Clients must have a job offer and be unable to afford items prior to first paycheck being issued. 1755 13th Street SE Salem, OR 97302 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday/Saturday 9am-noon, closed holidays Email (503) 364-9936 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES OUTREACH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides street outreach and individual case management for youth and young adults. Offers support, case management, and goal-setting tools to help identify and work towards future goals. Financial help and referrals to obtain interview clothes, laundry, obtain a birth certificate, Social Security card, ID, CPR/First Aid, or Food Handlers certification. Family mediation is available for families needing to work through a specific conflict. 225 SW 4th Street Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: Ages 10-24. Individuals from outside the service area will not be turned away. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-7pm, Saturday-Sunday by appointment Email (800) 901-2904 Get Directions Visit Website More Details JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES YOUTH SERVICE CENTER AND DROP IN OUTREACH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a drop in center with street outreach and individual case management. Offers support, resources, referral help, and physical items. Case management and goal setting can be set up to help an individual name and work towards future goals. Family mediation is also available for families needing to work through a specific conflict. Financial help and referrals for youth needing to do laundry, obtain interview clothes, or obtain a birth certificate, Social Security card, ID, CPR/First Aid, or Food Handlers certification is also available. 1025 Pacific Boulevard SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Ages 10-24. Individuals from outside the service area will not be turned away. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-7pm, Saturday-Sunday by appointment Email (800) 901-2904 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LOVE INC OF LINN COUNTY DRESS FOR SUCCESS CAREER CLOSET Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers clothing to job seekers for job interviews and starting new careers. 1606 Santiam Road SE Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Low-income households Hours: Monday/Wednesday/Thursday 9am-1pm, Tuesday 5pm-8pm Email (541) 924-5683 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARLA'S MALL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a thrift store with books, toys, household items, new hygiene products, knick-knacks, and clothing including interview or work-appropriate attire. Offers volunteer work experience and credit recovery for students working at the store. Also accepts donations of new and gently used items. 2500 E Street Baker City, OR 97814 Eligibility: Thrift Store: Unrestricted Volunteers: Baker School District high school students Hours: School year: Monday-Thursday 8am-11am and 1pm-4pm, closed holidays. Hours are subject to change. Summer hours: June 16th-July 17th Monday-Thursday 8:30am-12:30pm Email (541) 524-2309 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PROFESSIONAL WOMEN'S CLOTHES CLOSET CLOTHING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides shoes and clothing for women seeking professional apparel for work. Clothing donations accepted. 14700 SE Rupert Drive Oak Grove, OR 97267 Eligibility: Women in need of clothing and shoes Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-noon and 1pm-3pm Email (503) 654-3161 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SALVATION ARMY CASCADE DIVISION ROSEBURG CLOTHING AND HOUSEHOLD GOODS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing vouchers once every 12 months for children and adults. Vouchers are also provided for household items. 3130 NE Stephens Street Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Clothing vouchers: Unrestricted Household items vouchers: For those that have had fires or first time getting into a place after being homeless. Hours: Tuesday-Friday 9am-noon Clothing vouchers: Tuesday/Thursday 10am-noon Household vouchers: Appointment only Email (541) 672-6581 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VINA MOSES CENTER VINA'S FREE THRIFT STORE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides clothing and shoes including interview-ready clothes, food, home goods, kitchenware (pots, pans, dish sets), and hygiene items. Workforce closet available with work boots and shoes, rain and safety gear. Shopping allowed once per month. Exceptions made for special circumstances. Accepts donations of food, clothing, bedding, linens, kitchenware, toys and games, and hygiene supplies. 968 NW Garfield Avenue Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: -Thrift Store: Monday/Wednesday 1pm-3pm, Tuesday/Thursday 10am-noon and 1pm-3pm, 1st Saturday of the month noon-3pm -Donations: Visit website for current hours Email (541) 753-1420 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DRESS FOR SUCCESS OREGON DRESS FOR SUCCESS OREGON ELEVATE HIM PROFESSIONAL CLOTHING AND HAIRCUTS GOODWILL INDUSTRIES OF LANE AND SOUTH COAST COUNTIES VETERAN CONNECTIONS HOMELESS VETERAN REINTEGRATION PROGRAM HELPING HANDS RESOURCES CLOTHES THAT WORK JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES OUTREACH JACKSON STREET YOUTH SERVICES OUTREACH LOVE INC OF LINN COUNTY DRESS FOR SUCCESS CAREER CLOSET MARLA'S MALL MARLA'S MALL PROFESSIONAL WOMEN'S CLOTHES CLOSET CLOTHING SERVICES SALVATION ARMY CASCADE DIVISION ROSEBURG CLOTHING AND HOUSEHOLD GOODS VINA MOSES CENTER VINA'S FREE THRIFT STORE
